description: Someone is 'wiroa' when they see a desired outcome as possible. The expectation may be tentative. 'soliru' (hope) names hoping as an act or sustained orientation; 'pula' (wish) can desire an outcome without expecting it.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into high-front 'i'. Make a tap or short trill for 'r' and draw the tongue back into stressed true-mid rounded 'o'. After 'o', begin a fresh syllable for central 'a' by unrounding the lips and opening the jaw without inserting a glide.
usage_notes: The adjective predicates with 'nai' (be). A clause with 'soliru' (hope) can name the hoped-for outcome; 'wiroa' itself gives the person's present quality.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A solarpunk project needs people who can see changed conditions as possible before the work is finished. 'wiroa' names that anticipation.

semantic_domains.emotion: felt expectation that a desired outcome remains possible
semantic_domains.temporal: anticipation directed towards a future condition
